name: dnscrypt-proxy

pricing.details: Free and open source. Self-hosted. Works with any DNSCrypt or DoH-compatible resolver.

description: Local DNS proxy that encrypts DNS traffic and forwards it to a configurable upstream resolver. Supports DNSCrypt, DoH, and Anonymized DNSCrypt. Requires command-line configuration.
rationale: dnscrypt-proxy provides maximum flexibility and control over DNS encryption. The 'Anonymized DNSCrypt' mode routes queries through a relay, preventing the upstream resolver from seeing the client's IP address — a stronger privacy property than standard DoH/DoT. Configuration requires editing TOML files, which has a high usability barrier. Best suited for technically experienced users who want full DNS stack control without relying on a commercial provider.
